papers.summary: We construct a cellular sheaf from any feedforward ReLU neural network by placing one vertex for each intermediate quantity in the forward pass and encoding each computational step - affine transformation, activation, output - as a restriction map on an edge. The restricted coboundary operator on the free coordinates is unitriangular, so its determinant is $1$ and the restricted Laplacian is positive definite for every activation pattern. It follows that the relative cohomology vanishes and the forward pass output is the unique harmonic extension of the boundary data. The sheaf heat equation converges exponentially to this output despite the state-dependent switching introduced by piecewise linear activations. Unlike the forward pass, the heat equation propagates information bidirectionally across layers, enabling pinned neurons that impose constraints in both directions, training through local discrepancy minimization without a backward pass, and per-edge diagnostics that decompose network behavior by layer and operation type. We validate the framework experimentally on small synthetic tasks, confirming the convergence theorems and demonstrating that sheaf-based training, while not yet competitive with stochastic gradient descent, obeys quantitative scaling laws predicted by the theory.

papers.title: Connected graphs minimizing the spectral radius for given order and dissociation number
papers.summary: A dissociation set in a graph is a subset of vertices which induces a subgraph with maximum degree at most one. The dissociation number of a graph is the maximum cardinality of its dissociation sets. In this paper, we consider the $n$-vertex connected graphs with a given dissociation number that attain the minimum spectral radius. By using structure analysis and constructing difference equations, we characterize the extremal graphs with dissociation number $n-3$.

papers.title: Partially Hyperbolic Dynamics on $\mathbb T^4$: Existence of Compact Center-Unstable Leaves
papers.summary: We show that for $n\ge2$, if a partially hyperbolic diffeomorphism $f:\mathbb T^{n+1}\to \mathbb T^{n+1}$ with $\dim E^s=\dim E^c=1$ has an invariant center-unstable foliation with a compact incompressible leaf, then this foliation has a transverse closed curve in the universal cover. Also, if $f$ is leaf conjugate to its linear part, it has no compact incompressible center-unstable submanifold. In particular, by the incompressibility result we obtained on Anosov tori, the incompressibility assumptions can be removed when $f$ is defined on $\mathbb T^4$.

papers.title: Applications of Intuitionistic Temporal Logic to Temporal Answer Set Programming
papers.summary:
The relationship between intuitionistic or intermediate logics and logic programming has been extensively studied,
  prominently featuring Pearce's equilibrium logic and Osorio's safe beliefs.
  Equilibrium logic admits a fixpoint characterization based on the logic of here-and-there,
  akin to theory completion in default and autoepistemic logics.
  Safe beliefs are similarly defined via a fixpoint operator,
  albeit under the semantics of intuitionistic or other intermediate logics.
  In this paper,
  we investigate the logical foundations of Temporal Answer Set Programming through the lens of Temporal Equilibrium Logic,
  a formalism combining equilibrium logic with linear-time temporal operators.
  We lift the seminal approaches of Pearce and Osorio to the temporal setting,
  establishing a formal correspondence between temporal intuitionistic logic and temporal logic programming.
  Our results deepen the theoretical underpinnings of Temporal Answer Set Programming and
  provide new avenues for research in temporal reasoning.

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16004v1.title: 逆応答戦略と単調トラップを用いたパターン回避順列ゲーム
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16004v1.summary: 不偏ゲームの一種である PAP (Permutations Avoiding Patterns) において、プレイヤーが交互に回避すべきパターンを選択するルールを研究している。本論文では、長さ k のパターン集合 Bₖ を定義し、それが Sₖ における唯一の最小な単調強制部分集合であることを示した。すなわち、Bₖ を回避する十分に長い順列はすべて単調増加または単調減少であり、他のいかなる単調強制部分集合も Bₖ を含む。単調強制閾値の二次上界を証明し、k=3, 4, 5, 6 の場合の正確な閾値を決定した。さらに Bₖ の性質を利用し、k=4 かつ n ≥ 10 の場合に逆応答戦略 (reverse-reply strategy) が PAP で勝利することを示した。この戦略は、k と n が十分に大きい場合に常に勝利戦略となると推測されている。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16004v1.appendix: この論文は、順列の組み合わせ論やゲーム理論を扱っており、興味ある分野のうち combinatorics on words に関連する性質を含んでいます。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15864v2.title: リース積とその中心拡張の1階属について
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15864v2.summary: 整数環 Z の直積のリース積 Zᵐ wr Zⁿ が Z と正則に双解釈可能であることを示し、それらが1階述語論理において硬性的（first-order rigid）であることを証明した。具体的には、Zᵐ wr Zⁿ と初等同量な任意の有限生成群は Zᵐ wr Zⁿ と同型になる。一方で、Z² wr Z は、初等同量かつ互いに非同型な有限核を持つ中心拡張を連続体濃度（2^ℵ₀）個持つことを示した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15864v2.appendix: 興味ある分野の「structure of large countable infinite group」に関連する内容を含んでいます。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15830v1.title: ネックレス、部分集合和、および巡回置換
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15830v1.summary: 奇数 n に対し、{1, 2, ..., n} の部分集合のうち要素の和が n で割り切れるものの個数が、長さ n のバイナリネックレスの個数に等しいという既知の結果を二つの方向に一般化する。第一に、部分集合和が n を法として r に合同であるという条件を導入し、これがネックレスの周期性条件に対応することを示した。第二に、部分集合のサイズ k をネックレス内の 1 の数に対応させることで関係を精緻化した。特に r=1 の場合、部分集合和が 1 mod n である集合と、一つのサイクルからなる単峰置換を関連付ける Baker らの予想を母関数を用いて証明した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15830v1.appendix: 本論文はネックレス（円環状の単語）を扱っており、興味ある分野のうち「combinatorics on words」に深く関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15559v1.title: Storm による確率論的モデル検査
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15559v1.summary: 本論文は、確率論的モデル検査ツール「Storm」を用いた実践的なチュートリアルである。Storm は高いパフォーマンスと強力な Python エコシステムを特徴とし、多様なワークフローへの統合が容易である。本稿では、多くの分野で利用されるマルコフ決定過程 (MDP) に焦点を当て、Python によるモデリングから、多様な特性の検証、そして方策 (policy) の抽出に至るまでの基本的なワークフローを解説する。さらに、区間マルコフ決定過程 (interval MDP) や部分観測マルコフ決定過程 (POMDP) といった不確実性を扱う最新トピックや、既存のデータ構造を用いたアルゴリズムの実装についても紹介する。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15559v1.appendix: 本論文は、モデル検査の基礎理論として「automata theory」や「formal languages」と密接に関連しています。また、不確実性を伴うモデルの検証は、計算の信頼性の観点から「validated numerics」とも関連する可能性があります。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15549v1.title: 10回対称菱形タイル貼りにおける認識可能な置換規則
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15549v1.summary: 10回回転対称性を持つ菱形タイル貼り（Rhomb Tiling）に対する置換規則を提示する。このタイル貼りはペンローズ・タイリングと密接に関連しており、ペンタグリッド（pentagrid）構成法から得られる。本研究では、有限個の印付きプロトタイルを導入し、拡大係数が φ³ である具体的な置換規則を記述した。主要な成果として、この置換規則が「認識可能（recognizable）」であることを示し、タイル貼りの階層構造が局所的な配置から一意に復元できることを明らかにした。最後に、このタイル貼りとペンタグリッド構成法の関係性についても詳述している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15549v1.appendix: この論文は、タイリングの階層構造や置換規則を扱っており、組合せ論的な性質を持つことから、興味ある分野のうち「symbolic dynamics」や「combinatorics on words」に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15543v1.title: 有向Dutch windmillグラフのDrazin逆行列とウォーク構造
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15543v1.summary: 本論文では、有向Dutch windmillグラフと呼ばれるクラスの隣接行列におけるDrazin逆行列の性質を調査している。特定の長さのウォークの解析と最小多項式の構造を利用することで、Drazin逆行列の具体的な明示式を導出し、そのインデックスを決定した。この手法は組合せ論的な数え上げと代数的な行列解析を組み合わせたものであり、パス、サイクル、二部グラフに関する既知の結果を一般化している。本研究の枠組みは、周期的フィードバックを持つ離散モデルに洞察を与えるとともに、構造化ネットワークにおける一般化逆行列の記号計算の基礎となり得るものである。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15543v1.appendix: 本論文は「combinatorics on words」に関連する組合せ論的手法を用いており、また、構造化ネットワークにおける記号計算（symbolic computation）への言及があることから、計算機科学に関連する分野との接点を持っています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15529v1.title: アフィン・コクセター複体における Annexes
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15529v1.summary: コクセター群の元 x に対し、Bruhat 順序において x ≤ y を満たさない元 y の集合として定義される「Annex」という概念を導入した。この概念は、Bruhat 区間の研究や folded galleries による解釈を補完する視点を提供する。本論文では Annex の一般的な性質を確立し、アフィン・コクセター群において任意の固定された元の Annex が有限集合であることを示した。さらに、ランク 2 のアフィン・コクセター複体における Annex の境界の幾何学的構造を、降下集合や平行な鏡映の配置を用いて記述している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15529v1.appendix: 本論文はアフィン・コクセター群という無限群の構造を扱っているため、「structure of large countable infinite group」および組合せ論的な性質から「combinatorics on words」の分野に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15466v1.title: 漸近値の分岐に対する普遍的なモデル
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15466v1.summary: 本論文では、多項式的写像（polynomial-like maps）の超越的な類似物である「正接的写像（tangent-like maps）」の概念を研究している。自由な漸近値を1つだけ持つ、二次多項式に対応するモデル族を導入し、マンデルブロ集合の類似として「タンデルブロ集合（Tandelbrot set）」を定義した。充填ジュリア集合が連結な場合のモデル写像の一意性を含む、正接的写像に対するストレートニング定理（Straightening Theorem）と、そのパラメータ版を証明した。その帰結として、多くの有理型写像の分岐軌跡において、タンデルブロ集合の位相的なコピーが存在することを証明した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15466v1.appendix: 本論文は「complex dynamics」の分野に深く関連しています。多項式的写像の理論を複素力学系における有理型写像などの超越的な設定へと拡張し、分岐集合の構造を議論しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15451v1.title: 量子化された有理チップファイアリング
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15451v1.summary: 本論文は、有理格子路（rational lattice paths）および有理パーキング関数（rational parking functions）の理論と密接な関連を持つ「量子化されたチップファイアリングモデル」を導入している。このモデルでは、互いに素な正の整数 a, b と整数 c を用いて、頂点がシンクへ c 個、隣接頂点へ a/b 個のチップを送り出すルールで発火するが、各頂点におけるチップの増減が整数値に切り捨て（round down）されるという特徴を持つ。著者らは本モデルにおいて superstable、k-stable、k-skeletal という構成概念を定義し、完全グラフかつ c = 1 の場合に superstable な構成が有理パーキング関数に対応することを示した。また、k-skeletal な有理格子路の組合せ論的理論を構築することで、superstable な構成と k-skeletal な構成の間の全単射を確立した。さらに、チップ構成のなす群が (ℤ/aℤ) の b - 1 個の直積に同型であること、および各剰余類に一意な k-skeletal な代表元が存在することを証明した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15451v1.appendix: 本論文は有理格子路やパーキング関数を扱っており、興味ある分野の combinatorics に関連しています。チップファイアリングはグラフ上の離散力学系の一種としても解釈可能です。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15716v1.title: 有界解の剛性と構造的非対称性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15716v1.summary: 本論文では、複素パラメータ s に依存する [1, ∞) 上の非斉次線形複素微分方程式の族を導入する。非斉次項に関する「回転数仮説 (Rotation number hypothesis)」を特定し、構造的非対称性を確立した。クリティカル・ストリップ内のパラメータ s および 1-s̅ に対応し、初期条件を 1 とする 2 つの解がいずれも [1, +∞) において有界であるならば、Re(s) = 1/2 が成り立つことを示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15716v1.appendix: 複素パラメータに依存する微分方程式の動的な性質や構造を議論していることから、複素力学系 (complex dynamics) の分野に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15379v3.title: 計量時相論理 (MTL) の表現力の再考："Je t'aime, moi non plus" の物語
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15379v3.summary: 本論文では、計量時相論理 (MTL) の表現力に関する従来の定説に再考を迫る。過去20年間の研究では、インターバルに基づく意味論がポイントワイズな意味論よりも厳密に表現力が高いとされてきた。これは、定時イベントの位置ではなく任意の時間点で論理式を評価することで表現力が増すことを示唆していた。しかし、本研究は有限または非ゼノな無限実行モデルにおいて、これら二つの意味論が実際には比較不能であることを証明し、長年の定説を覆した。さらに、両方の意味論を包含する新しい「混合」意味論を提案している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15379v3.appendix: 本論文は興味ある分野のうち「automata theory」および「formal languages」に関連しています。時間制約を持つシステムの形式的検証やオートマトン理論の基礎となる論理の表現力について論じています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15314v1.title: 放物型部分群へのレトラクションを許容するArtin群の分類
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15314v1.summary: すべての放物型部分群へのレトラクションを持つArtin群の分類を行った。三角形部分群の解析に基づき、二面体Artin群間の準同型写像（一方の標準生成元を標準生成元に写すもの）の分類を核としている。その結果、放物型部分群へのレトラクションを持つArtin群は、各標準生成元を別の標準生成元または単位元へ写すような「通常の」レトラクションも必ず持つことが示された。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15314v1.appendix: 本研究はArtin群の構造や部分群の関係を記述しており、興味ある分野の中では「structure of large countable infinite group」に関連があります。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15264v1.title: Coarse median と普遍 quasigeodesic cone
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15264v1.summary: 一様な coarse median 空間の普遍 quasigeodesic cone が、標準的な coarse median 構造を持つことを示した。この結果の応用として、階層的双曲空間 (hierarchically hyperbolic spaces) が、双曲因子空間への射影と整合的な coarse median 構造を持つという Bowditch の既知の結果を再導出した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15264v1.appendix: この論文は幾何学的群論における重要な概念を扱っており、興味ある分野のうち「structure of large countable infinite group」に深く関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15177v1.title: 半群準同型の既約性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15177v1.summary: アルファベット Σ 上の正の自由半群における準同型 φ: Σ⁺ → Σ⁺ の既約性という概念を導入し、その性質を研究している。準同型 φ が既約であるとは、任意の分解 φ = ψ₂ ∘ ψ₁ において ψ₁ または ψ₂ が自明な準同型である場合に限られることを指し、それ以外の場合は可約（reducible）とされる。本論文では既約性の特徴付けを行い、この概念に関する一連の基礎的な問題を考察している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15177v1.appendix: 本研究は、語の組合せ論 (combinatorics on words) および形式言語理論 (formal languages) に密接に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15099v1.title: Cylindric Algebraを経由した関係代数の完全性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15099v1.summary: 一階述語論理の許容式（allowed formulas）に対する関係代数の完全性について、代数的な手法を用いた新しい証明を提示している。この証明は関係代数をCylindric Algebra（円筒代数）に埋め込む手法に基づいており、より代数的なアプローチで完全性を確立している。また、与えられた許容式と等価な関係代数式を生成するアルゴリズムも提案されている。本研究の主な動機は、不完全な情報や曖昧な情報を扱う関係モデルへの一般化に適した完全性の証明を構築することにある。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15099v1.appendix: 提示された「興味ある分野」との直接的な関連性はありませんが、数理論理学および代数的構造の観点から計算機科学の基礎理論を扱う内容です。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15098v1.title: 平面における一意化問題：サーベイ
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15098v1.summary: 複素平面における（準）等角一意化問題の歴史と近年の進展をまとめたサーベイ論文である。中心的な話題は、任意の平面領域が円領域（補集合が点または閉円板である領域）へ等角に写像可能であると予測する、120年近く未解決の「Koebeの予想」である。本稿では、この予想の歴史、最新の進展、および一意性の問題を概説する。また、平面の準等角自己同相写像によってコンパクト集合をSchottky集合（補集合が互いに素な開円板の和集合である集合）へ写像できるかという派生的な問題についても議論している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15098v1.appendix: 本論文で扱われている（準）等角写像や複素平面上の一意化の議論は、興味ある分野に含まれる complex dynamics（複素力学系）と密接に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15091v1.title: 信頼性の高いクープマン作用素学習：不変性の診断と誤差評価
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15091v1.summary: クープマン作用素理論は非線形力学系の非線形性を線形表現として扱うための基盤であるが、実用上の有限次元特徴空間では不変性が保たれないことが多く、射影誤差によって偽の固有値や誤った予測が生じるという課題がある。本論文は、スナップショットデータのみを用いて特徴空間の不変性と射影誤差を定量化し、近似の信頼性を保証する事後検証手法を提案している。具体的には、部分空間とそのクープマン像の間の主角を用いた「主角分解（PAD）」を導入し、RKHSに基づく多段階誤差評価やガウス過程による誤差予測モデルを導出している。これにより、カオス系や高次元データにおけるスペクトル解析や予測の精度向上が可能となる。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15091v1.appendix: 本論文は、非線形力学系やカオス系の解析を対象としているため「complex dynamics」に関連しており、また数値計算結果の信頼性を担保するための事後的な誤差評価手法を提供している点から「validated numerics」の分野と関連があります。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15073v1.title: 解析学における計算機援用証明の進化
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15073v1.summary: 本論文は、Neural ODEや物理学に基づいたニューラルネットワーク（PINN）などで用いられる連続流の離散近似において、硬い系（stiff systems）で発生する数値的分岐と擬似アトラクタの問題を分析している。Heun法を用いた2次元非線形系において、離散写像が真の平衡点を不安定化させ、存在しない周期4のシンク（spurious period-4 sink）を生じさせることを示した。この現象を証明するために、論文内に直接計算機援用証明（CAP）を組み込むリテレートプログラミングの手法を採用し、複素区間演算と「Snap-to-Axis」射影を用いて、特定の軌道が擬似的な周期解に永久に捕捉されることを厳密に検証している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.15073v1.appendix: 本論文は「validated numerics（精度保証付き数値計算）」および「complex dynamics（複素力学系）」の分野と密接に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14999v1.title: 非周期的構造は決して崩壊しない：ロスレス圧縮のためのフィボナッチ階層
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14999v1.summary: 本論文は、ロスレスデータ圧縮における非周期的階層（特にフィボナッチ準結晶タイル貼り）の優位性を理論と実践の両面から示している。周期的な階層が有限の深さで「崩壊」し辞書の再利用性が消失するのに対し、フィボナッチ階層は全スケールでスケール不変なカバレッジを維持する「黄金補償（Golden Compensation）」特性を持つ。解析の結果、Sturmian複雑度の法則に従い符号語のカバレッジ効率が最大化されること、および長距離依存性下で周期階層より低いエントロピーを達成することが明らかになった。実装された圧縮器「Quasicryth」を用いた実験では、データサイズが大きくなるほど非周期的階層の優位性が拡大し、enwik9データセットにおいて準結晶タイル貼り自体が顕著な圧縮性能向上に寄与することが確認された。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14999v1.appendix: 本論文は、ワード上の組合せ論（combinatorics on words）におけるSturmian語の性質をデータ圧縮に適用しており、symbolic dynamicsやformal languagesの分野と密接に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14862v1.title: 負のβ変換：不変測度、有限型部分シフト、およびマッチング特性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14862v1.summary: β > 1 および x ∈ (0,1] における負のβ変換 T₋ᵦ(x) = -βx + ⌊βx⌋ + 1 についての研究である。本論文では、2つの異なる非整数 β₁, β₂ が同一の T₋ᵦ 不変測度を持つための必要十分条件が、β₁ が方程式 x² - qx - p = 0 (p, q ∈ ℕ, p ≤ q) の根であり、かつ β₂ = β₁ + 1 であることを示した。また、一般化 multinacci 数において T₋ᵦ がマッチング特性を持つことを証明した。さらに、-β-シフトが有限型部分シフトとなるような単純 -β 数が、パラメータ区間 (1, ∞) において稠密であることを明らかにした。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14862v1.appendix: この論文は、記号力学系 (symbolic dynamics) におけるシフト空間の性質や変換の不変測度を扱っており、指定された興味ある分野と強く関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14692v1.title: 直観主義時相論理の時相回答セットプログラミングへの応用
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14692v1.summary: 本論文では、直観主義論理や中間論理と論理プログラミングの関係性、特にPearceの均衡論理やOsorioのsafe beliefsといった既存手法を背景に、時相回答セットプログラミング（TASP）の論理的基礎を考察している。線形時間時相演算子を組み込んだ時相均衡論理（TEL）を用い、PearceやOsorioのアプローチを時相設定へと拡張した。これにより、時相直観主義論理と時相論理プログラミングの間の形式的な対応関係を確立し、TASPの理論的基盤を強化するとともに、時相推論における新たな研究の方向性を提示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.14692v1.appendix: 本論文は、時相論理の論理的性質を扱う点で「automata theory」や「formal languages」の分野と深い関連があります。特に、時相論理の充足可能性やモデル検査の理論的基盤において、これらの分野の手法が頻繁に用いられます。

papers.title: Sequential densities of rational languages
papers.summary: We introduce the notion of density of a rational language with respect to a sequence of probability measures. We prove that if $(μ_n)$ is a sequence of Bernoulli measures converging to a positive Bernoulli measure $\overlineμ$, the sequential density is the ordinary density with respect to $\overlineμ$. We also prove that if $(μ_n)$ is a sequence of invariant probability measures converging in the strong sense to an invariant probability measure $\overlineμ$, then the sequential density of every rational language exists for this sequence.

papers.title: Coded Information Retrieval for Block-Structured DNA-Based Data Storage
papers.summary: We study the problem of coded information retrieval for block-structured data, motivated by DNA-based storage systems where a database is partitioned into multiple files that must each be recoverable as an atomic unit. We initiate and formalize the block-structured retrieval problem, wherein $k$ information symbols are partitioned into two files $F_1$ and $F_2$ of sizes $s_1$ and $s_2 = k - s_1$. The objective is to characterize the set of achievable expected retrieval time pairs $\bigl(E_1(G), E_2(G)\bigr)$ over all $[n,k]$ linear codes with generator matrix $G$. We derive a family of linear lower bounds via mutual exclusivity of recovery sets, and develop a nonlinear geometric bound via column projection. For codes with no mixed columns, this yields the hyperbolic constraint $s_1/E_1 + s_2/E_2 \le 1$, which we conjecture to hold universally whenever $\max\{s_1,s_2\} \ge 2$. We analyze explicit codes, such as the identity code, file-dedicated MDS codes, and the systematic global MDS code, and compute their exact expected retrieval times. For file-dedicated codes we prove MDS optimality within the family and verify the hyperbolic constraint. For global MDS codes, we establish dominance by the proportional local MDS allocation via a combinatorial subset-counting argument, providing a significantly simpler proof compared to recent literature and formally extending the result to the asymmetric case. Finally, we characterize the limiting achievability region as $n \to \infty$: the hyperbolic boundary is asymptotically achieved by file-dedicated MDS codes, and is conjectured to be the exact boundary of the limiting achievability region.

papers.title: Sieving with square conditions and applications to Hilbert cubes in arithmetic sets
papers.summary: The purpose of this paper is twofold: 1) Applications of Gallagher's larger sieve modulo prime squares do not work. In some relevant cases we can transform the residue class information modulo $p^2$ to more suitable residue information modulo $p$, so that we can successfully apply the sieve. 2) The applications to Hilbert cubes are of interest in their own right: We study the maximal dimension of Hilbert cubes in various multiplicatively defined sets. For the squareful numbers in $[1,N]$ we achieve an upper bound of the dimension of $d=O(\log N)$. The same upper bounds follow for multiplicative semigroups of integers defined by a positive proportion of the primes, and the set of integers representable by an irreducible positive definite binary quadratic form. Eventually, making use of the sun flower lemma we give an improvement on the maximal dimension $d$ of subset sums in the set of pure powers in $[1,N]$.

papers.title: Orthogonality between acyclic subdigraphs and paths in digraphs
papers.summary:
Let $D$ be a digraph. A collection of disjoint sets of vertices (respec., collection of disjoint subdigraphs) $\mathcal{H}$ of $D$ and a vertex subset (or subdigraph) $Q$ of $D$ are orthogonal if every set (respec., subdigraph) $H \in \mathcal{H}$ contains exactly one vertex of $Q$. A well-known result of Gallai and Milgram shows that for every minimum path partition of a digraph there is a stable set orthogonal to it. Similarly, Gallai, Hasse, Roy and Vitaver independently proved that for every longest path of a digraph there is a vertex partition into stable sets (i.e, vertex-coloring) orthogonal to it. Berge showed that no analogous statements hold when optimality is required for the stable set or the vertex coloring. In this paper, we show that this holds if we replace stable sets by induced acyclic subdigraphs.
  In 1981, Linial proposed two generalizations of Gallai-Milgram and Gallai-Hasse-Roy-Vitaver results using a positive integer $k$ as a measure of optimality for the path partition and the coloring, respectively. These generalizations have led to two conjectures that remain open. Using the same strategy of replacing stable sets by induced acyclic subdigraphs, we prove relaxations of both conjectures.

papers.title: Closing Trees into Unicyclic Counterexamples
papers.summary: We develop a family-based route to unicyclic graphs whose independence polynomials are unimodal but not log-concave. The paper is organized around one flagship statement: for the explicit KL-closure family $U_{k,r}$, with $r\in\{0,1,2\}$ and admissible $k$, the independence polynomial is unimodal but not log-concave. The proof separates the closure polynomial into a dominant convolution term and a real-rooted correction term. On the non-log-concavity side, we prove symbolically that the penultimate log-concavity inequality fails for every admissible parameter. On the unimodality side, we prove that the main convolution term $H_{k,r}=G_kF_{k+r}$ is unimodal with a controlled mode, using a combination of exact coefficient formulas, Ibragimov's strong-unimodality principle, and a residue-class growth argument. Darroch localization and an adjacent-mode bridge lemma then transfer that mode statement to the full KL closure polynomial. This yields an explicit infinite family of unicyclic graphs with unimodal but non-log-concave independence polynomials. In the exact range $k\le 400$, we further verify that the penultimate break is unique and determine exact mode formulas for $H_{k,r}$, the binomial correction term, and $I(U_{k,r};x)$ itself. The paper also places the KL family inside a broader reservoir program involving Galvin, Ramos-Sun, and Bautista-Ramos trees, from which we obtain substantial universal exact theorems for finite ranges.

papers.title: A Brooks-type theorem for the k-choosability of graphs with maximum local edge-connectivity k
papers.summary: For a graph $G$ with at least two vertices, the maximum local edge-connectivity of $G$ is the maximum number of edge-disjoint $(u,v)$-paths over all distinct pairs of vertices $(u,v)$ in $G$. Stiebitz and Toft (2018) proved a Brooks-type theorem for graphs with maximum local edge-connectivity $k$, showing that a graph with maximum local edge-connectivity $k$ is not $k$-colourable if and only if it has a block in $\mathcal{H}_k$, which is the class of graphs that can be obtained by taking Hajós joins of copies of $K_{k+1}$ and, when $k=3$, odd wheels. We prove that a $2$-connected graph with maximum local edge-connectivity $k$ is $k$-choosable if and only if it is not in $\mathcal{H}_k$. On the other hand, deciding $k$-choosability when restricted to graphs with maximum local edge-connectivity $k$ (that might not be $2$-connected) is $Π_2$-complete. To prove the former result, we first prove several generalisations of a well-known characterisation of degree-choosability; these may be of independent interest.

papers.title: On quantization and the classical variational principle for the metric mean dimension
papers.summary: For a homeomorphism $T\colon X \to X$ on a compact metric space $(X,d)$, one can find in the literature several distinct notions of measure-theoretic $\varepsilon$-entropy maps $F(μ,\varepsilon)$, defined on the space of Borel $T$-invariant probability measures of $X$, satisfying $$ \mathrm{\overline{mdim}_M}(X,d,T) \, =\, \limsup_{\varepsilon \, \to \, 0^+}\, \sup_{μ\,\in\, P_T(X)} \, \frac{F(μ,\varepsilon)}{\log\,(1/\varepsilon)} $$ where $\mathrm{\overline{mdim}_M}(X,d,T)$ stands for the upper metric mean dimension. A major question regards the change in the order in which $\limsup_{\varepsilon}$ and $\sup_{μ\,\in\, P_T(X)}$ appear in the previous equality. We introduce the notion of mean quantization dimension of a measure and prove that $$ \mathrm{\overline{mdim}_M}(X,d,T) \,\, =\, \max_{μ\,\in\, P_T(X)} \, \mathrm{\overline{mdim}_Q}(μ). $$ This concept exhibits a fundamental property that prompted our search for sufficient conditions on $F$ under which the aforementioned change in order is valid. We show that some of the well-known maps $F$ do not satisfy this property and fail to comply with a classical variational principle.

papers.title: On the model theory of the Free Factor Complex of rank 2
papers.summary: We begin the investigation of the free factor complex of a free group of finite rank. For the case of rank 2 we axiomatize its theory and show that it is $ω$-stable with prime model $AF_2$.

papers.title: A family of Neumaier graphs containing examples with exactly five eigenvalues
papers.summary: A Neumaier graph is an edge-regular graph with a regular clique. Such a graph is said to have parameters $(v,k,λ;e,s)$ if it is a $k$-regular graph on $v$ vertices having a clique of size $s$ such that every edge is contained in $λ$ triangles and every vertex outside $C$ is adjacent with exactly $e$ vertices inside $C$. It was an open problem whether Neumaier graphs can exist with exactly five eigenvalues. In the present paper, we describe a family of Neumaier graphs, and show that inside this family there are 1063 nonisomorphic Neumaier graphs with parameters $(v,k,λ;e,s)=(48,14,2;1,4)$, among which 25 have exactly five eigenvalues. These 1063 graphs are also the first known examples of Neumaier graphs for the mentioned parameters.

papers.title: Unifying Optimization and Dynamics to Parallelize Sequential Computation: A Guide to Parallel Newton Methods for Breaking Sequential Bottlenecks
papers.summary: Massively parallel hardware (GPUs) and long sequence data have made parallel algorithms essential for machine learning at scale. Yet dynamical systems, like recurrent neural networks and Markov chain Monte Carlo, were thought to suffer from sequential bottlenecks. Recent work showed that dynamical systems can in fact be parallelized across the sequence length by reframing their evaluation as a system of nonlinear equations, which can be solved with Newton's method using a parallel associative scan. However, these parallel Newton methods struggled with limitations, primarily inefficiency, instability, and lack of convergence guarantees. This thesis addresses these limitations with methodological and theoretical contributions, drawing particularly from optimization. Methodologically, we develop scalable and stable parallel Newton methods, based on quasi-Newton and trust-region approaches. The quasi-Newton methods are faster and more memory efficient, while the trust-region approaches are significantly more stable. Theoretically, we unify many fixed-point methods into our parallel Newton framework, including Picard and Jacobi iterations. We establish a linear convergence rate for these techniques that depends on the method's approximation accuracy and stability. Moreover, we give a precise condition, rooted in dynamical stability, that characterizes when parallelization provably accelerates a dynamical system and when it cannot. Specifically, the sign of the Largest Lyapunov Exponent of a dynamical system determines whether or not parallel Newton methods converge quickly. In sum, this thesis unlocks scalable and stable methods for parallelizing sequential computation, and provides a firm theoretical basis for when such techniques will and will not work. This thesis also serves as a guide to parallel Newton methods for researchers who want to write the next chapter in this ongoing story.

papers.title: Nearly-polynomial inverse theorem for the U^d norm in degree d+1
papers.summary: We prove a nearly polynomial inverse theorem for the Gowers $U^d$ norm, over finite fields of non-small characteristic, for polynomials of degree $d+1$. The case of degree $d$ was very recently settled by Milićević and Randelović with a fully polynomial bound. We moreover provide a nearly polynomial inverse theorem for homogeneous polynomials of any degree smaller than $2d$.

papers.title: Inadmissible representations of the tree automorphism group
papers.summary: The automorphism group of a regular locally finite tree is shown to admit irreducible Banach representations that are not admissible. The dense subspace of smooth vectors contains no algebraically irreducible component.

papers.title: Revisiting fully residually free Demushkin groups
papers.summary: We establish new examples and non-examples of pro-p limit groups among the class of Demushkin groups, that is, pro-p Poincaré duality groups of dimension 2.

papers.title: Computability of the Hahn-Banach Theorem Revisited
papers.summary: Computational properties of the Hahn-Banach theorem have been studied in computable, constructive and reverse mathematics and in all these approaches the theorem is equivalent to weak Kőnig's lemma. Gherardi and Marcone proved that this is also true in the uniform sense of Weihrauch complexity. However, their result requires the underlying space to be variable. We prove that the Hahn-Banach theorem attains its full complexity already for the Banach space $\ell^1$. We also prove that the one-step Hahn-Banach theorem for this space is Weihrauch equivalent to the intermediate value theorem. This also yields a new and very simple proof of the reduction of the Hahn-Banach theorem to weak Kőnig's lemma using infinite products. Finally, we show that the Hahn-Banach theorem for $\ell^1$ in the two-dimensional case is Weihrauch equivalent to the lesser limited principle of omniscience.

papers.title: Tree-like is not a transitive relation on paths
papers.summary: The notions of tree-like loop and Lipschitz tree-like loop were introduced by Hambly and Lyons in their 2010 Annals of Mathematics paper. They showed that the Lipschitz tree-like property determines an equivalence relation on the set of paths of bounded variation in a given metric space and then asked if this notion could be extended to paths without the Lipschitz requirement. We show that after eliminating the Lipschitz requirement, the resulting relation is no longer transitive and thus is not an equivalence relation. The counterexample is obtained by analyzing an explicit fractal construction in the plane.

papers.title: Commuting probability of skew left braces
papers.summary: We introduce a concept of the commuting probability of a skew left brace analogous to group theory. We establish upper and lower bounds for the commuting probability and prove that, for finite non-trivial skew left braces, it is always at most $\frac{3}{4}$. Interestingly, there is no skew left brace with commuting probability in the open interval $(5/8, 1)$, except $\frac{3}{4}$, for which we construct an explicit example. A characterization of skew left braces having commuting probability $\frac{3}{4}$ or $\frac{5}{8}$ is presented. We further show that the finite skew left braces with commuting probability larger than $\frac{65}{128}$ are necessarily nilpotent. We prove that the commuting probability remains invariant under isoclinism of skew braces. We introduce a concept of a compact Hausdorff topological skew left brace $B$, where we prove that the set of all elements of $B$ having finite centraliser index in $B$ is a Borel subgroup. For such infinite non-trivial skew left braces too $\frac{3}{4}$ is the upper bound for the commuting probability, and $\frac{3}{4}$ is the only rational number which occurs as commuting probability in the open interval $(5/8, 1)$.

papers.title: General multi-Novikov algebras, multi-differential algebras and their free constructions
papers.summary: Motivated by the recent development of noncommutative Novikov algebras and multi-Novikov algebras from the study of regularity structures of stochastic PDEs, this paper gives a general approach to study various multi-Novikov algebras and multi-differential algebras, with close connection with Poisson algebras. The construction of S. Gelfand of Novikov algebras from differential commutative algebras is generalized to this context. Free noncommuting multi-Novikov algebras are constructed from typed decorated rooted trees and from noncommuting multi-differential polynomials with populated conditions.

papers.title: Coherent RFRS groups
papers.summary:
We prove that a finitely generated virtually RFRS group of cohomological dimension at most $2$ is coherent if and only if its second $L^{2}$-Betti number vanishes if and only if it is virtually free-by-cyclic. The non-vanishing of the second $L^{2}$-Betti number provides the first known global obstruction to coherence in any reasonably wide class of groups, allowing for proofs of incoherence without needing to exhibit explicit witnesses to incoherence.
  As applications of this result, we completely characterise coherence among two-dimensional Coxeter groups, confirming conjectures of Jankiewicz and Wise, and show that incoherence is generic in groups of nonpositive deficiency, confirming a conjecture of Wise. We also find that, among virtually compact special groups of virtual cohomological dimension two, coherence is algorithmically decidable and is a quasi-isometry, measure equivalence, and profinite invariant.
  In an appendix, Marco Linton applies one of the main results to prove that cubulated locally quasi-convex hyperbolic groups are virtually free-by-cyclic, solving problems of Abdenbi--Wise and Wise in the cubulated case.

papers.title: Formal verification of tree-based machine learning models for lateral spreading
papers.summary: Machine learning models for geotechnical hazard prediction can achieve high accuracy while learning physically inconsistent relationships from sparse or biased training data. Current remedies (post-hoc explainability, such as SHAP and LIME, and training-time constraints) either diagnose individual predictions approximately or restrict model capacity without providing exhaustive guarantees. This paper encodes trained tree ensembles as logical formulas in a Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) solver and checks physical specifications across the entire input domain, not just sampled points. Four geotechnical specifications (water table depth, PGA monotonicity, distance safety, and flat-ground safety) are formalized as decidable logical formulas and verified via SMT against both XGBoost ensembles and Explainable Boosting Machines (EBMs) trained on the 2011 Christchurch earthquake lateral spreading dataset (7,291 sites, four features). The SMT solver either produces a concrete counterexample where a specification fails or proves that no violation exists. The unconstrained EBM (80.1% accuracy) violates all four specifications. A fully constrained EBM (67.2%) satisfies three of four specifications, demonstrating that iterative constraint application guided by verification can progressively improve physical consistency. A Pareto analysis of 33 model variants reveals a persistent trade-off, as none of the variants studied achieve both greater than 80% accuracy and full compliance with the specified set. SHAP analysis of specification counterexamples shows that the offending feature can rank last, demonstrating that post-hoc explanations do not substitute for formal verification. These results establish a verify-fix-verify engineering loop and a formal certification for deploying physically consistent ML models in safety-critical geotechnical applications.

papers.title: Data-driven forced response analysis with min-max representations of nonlinear restoring forces
papers.summary: This paper discusses a novel data-driven nonlinearity identification method for mechanical systems with nonlinear restoring forces such as polynomial, piecewise-linear, and general displacement-dependent nonlinearities. The proposed method is built upon the universal approximation theorem that states that a nonlinear function can be approximated by a linear combination of activation functions in artificial neural network framework. The proposed approach utilizes piecewise linear springs with initial gaps to act as the activation functions of the neurons of artificial neural networks. A library of piecewise linear springs with initial gaps are constructed, and the contributions of the springs on the nonlinear restoring force are determined by solving the linear regression problems. The piecewise linear springs are realized by combinations of min and max functions with biases. The proposed method is applied to a Duffing oscillator with cubic stiffness, and a piecewise linear oscillator with a gap and their nonlinearities are successfully determined from their free responses. The obtained models are then used for conducting forced response analysis and the results match well with those of the original system. The method is then applied to experimentally-obtained free response data of a cantilevered plate that is subjected to magnetic restoring force, and successfully finds the piecewise linear representation of the magnetic force. It is also shown that the obtained model is capable of accurately capturing the steady-state response of the system subject to harmonic base excitation.

papers.title: The recording tableaux of the quantum Littlewood-Richardson map and the orthogonal transpose symmetry map
papers.summary: Recently Watanabe has given an algorithm to compute a bijection, that he calls (quantum) Littlewood-Richardson (LR) map, between semi-standard Young tableaux of shape a partition with at most $2n$ parts and pairs of tableaux consisting of a symplectic tableau with shape a partition with at most $n$ parts, and a recording tableau of skew-shape given by the two previous shapes. The recording tableaux in that algorithm are shown to be equinumerous to Littlewood-Richardson-Sundaram tableaux whose injectivity is shown combinatorially while the surjectivity is concluded via representation theory of a quantum symmetric pair of type AII. Henceforth, the algorithm to compute the quantum LR map provides a new branching model for the branching multiplicities from $GL_{2n}(C)$ to $Sp_{2n}(C)$. Here, as morally suggested by Watanabe, one provides a combinatorial proof of the surjectivity of the quantum LR map which in turn exhibits the restriction of the LR orthogonal transpose symmetry map to LR-Sundaram tableaux.

papers.title: Independence polynomials of graphs
papers.summary: In this paper, we study the independence polynomial $P_G(x)$ of a finite simple graph $G$, with emphasis on the evaluation at $x=-1$, symmetry, and its connection with the $h$-polynomial of the edge ideal of $G$. For big star graphs, we determine exactly when $P_G(-1)$ is $0, 1$, or $-1$, characterize the pseudo-Gorenstein$^*$ members, and show that there is a unique big star with symmetric independence polynomial. We also study graphs obtained from a graph $H$ by attaching leaves to selected vertices. We derive an explicit formula for the resulting independence polynomial, determine the corresponding value at $-1$, and prove that if every vertex of $H$ receives at least one leaf, then the independence polynomial is symmetric if and only if each vertex receives exactly two leaves. As an application, we obtain exact criteria for the values of $P_G(-1)$ and for the pseudo-Gorenstein$^*$ members of caterpillar graphs. For cochordal graphs, we classify all symmetric independence polynomials. Finally, for connected graphs on $n$ vertices with small independence numbers, we determine the exact range of possible values of $P_G(-1)$.

papers.title: Profinite detection of free products and free factors
papers.summary: Let $G$ be the fundamental group of a graph of finitely generated virtually free groups with virtually cyclic edge groups. We shaw that $G$ is cohomologically good if $G$ is residually finite. If $G$ is LERF, we prove that G splits non-trivially as a free product if and only if its profinite completion $\widehat{G}$ splits non-trivially as a free profinite product. Moreover, we are able to detect one-ended free factors of $G$ from $\widehat{G}$. As an application, we deduce that any profinitely rigid word in a finitely generated free group is universally profinitely rigid.

papers.title: A quadratic-time coloring algorithm for graphs with large maximum degree
papers.summary: Graph coloring is a central problem in graph theory and is NP-hard for general graphs. Motivated by the Borodin--Kostochka conjecture, we study the algorithmic problem of coloring graphs with large maximum degree and no clique of size $Δ$. We give a quadratic-time coloring algorithm that constructs a $(Δ-1)$-coloring for such graphs. We also prove that every graph $G$ with maximum degree $Δ\ge 7.3 \times 10^9$ and clique number $ω(G) < Δ$ satisfies $χ(G) \le Δ- 1$. This improves a longstanding result of Reed.

papers.title: Trajectory Stability and Signature Diagnostics for Comet-Based Interstellar Navigation
papers.summary: Interstellar objects (ISOs) motivate a coupled mission-design and inference question relevant to spacecraft dynamics and control in extreme environments: if volatile-rich, rotating comet-like bodies were used for sustained deep-space navigation by exploiting pre-existing hyperbolic motion and in-situ propellant, what stability requirements arise under non-gravitational forcing, and what astrometric signatures might distinguish active stabilization from uncontrolled natural dynamics? We develop a stability-theoretic framework for trajectory tracking with jet-actuated correction, and show that high-speed transit geometry -- including debris-belt avoidance and encounter phasing -- tightly constrains feasible trajectories, making long-horizon tracking stability mission-critical. We model tracking residuals as the balance of disturbances and corrective action, and derive stability conditions across four levels: disturbance-energy stability, outer-loop contraction, actuator-memory stability, and rotation-mediated (Floquet) stability. The analysis implies residual diagnostics that can motivate empirical tests: under comparable forcing, effective stabilization is expected to strengthen short-horizon error correction, reduce event-conditioned persistence and variance clustering, regularize standardized innovations, and yield bounded post-shock recovery. More broadly, the framework provides a reference for deep-space guidance and control under nonlinear, multi-field disturbances and for planetary-defense concepts involving attitude shaping or impulsive kinetic impact.

papers.title: A characterization of terminal planar networks by forbidden structures
papers.summary: The class of terminal planar networks was recently introduced from a biological perspective in relation to the visualization of phylogenetic networks, and its connection to upward planar networks has been established. We provide a Kuratowski-type theorem that characterizes terminal planar networks by a finite set of forbidden structures, defined via six families of 0/1-labeled graphs. Another characterization based on planarity of supergraphs yields linear-time algorithms for testing terminal planarity and for computing such planar drawings. We describe an application that is potentially relevant in broader, non-phylogenetic settings. We also discuss a connection of our main result to an open problem on the forbidden structures of single-source upward planar networks.

papers.title: A Jacobi Field Approach to Splitting Detection in Schrödinger Bridge
papers.summary: We study the problem of detecting the onset of path splitting in stochastic interpolation between probability distributions. This question is especially subtle when the target distribution is nonconvex or supported on disconnected components, where interpolating trajectories may separate into distinct branches. Motivated by the stochastic control and Schrödinger bridge viewpoint, we propose a Jacobi field based indicator for identifying candidate splitting times and locations. Our approach is based on the Jacobi field associated with the linearization of an induced interpolating flow. Starting from a stochastic interpolation ansatz, we construct an Eulerian velocity field by conditional averaging and derive its spatial Jacobian in terms of the local posterior geometry of the target sample cloud. This allows us to interpret the symmetric part of the Jacobian as a local strain tensor and to use its spectral structure to quantify the amplification of infinitesimal perturbations along reference trajectories. Numerical experiments on non-convex and disconnected target distributions show that the proposed indicator consistently localizes the emergence of branching regions and captures the temporal development of splitting. These results suggest that Jacobi field analysis provides a natural mathematical framework for studying local instability and splitting phenomena in stochastic interpolation.

papers.title: Blow-up structure of graphs excluding a tree or an apex-tree as a minor
papers.summary:
We prove blow-up structure theorems for graphs excluding a tree or an apex-tree as a minor. First, we show that for every $t$-vertex tree $T$ with $t\geq 3$ and radius $h$, and every graph $G$ excluding $T$ as a minor, there exists a graph $H$ with pathwidth at most $2h-1$ such that $G$ is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{t-2}$ as a subgraph. This improves on a recent theorem of Dujmović, Hickingbotham, Joret, Micek, Morin, and Wood (2024), who proved the same result but with a larger bound on the order of the complete graph in the product.
  Second, we show that for every $t$-vertex tree $T$ with $t\geq 2$, radius $h$ and maximum degree $d$, and every graph $G$ excluding the apex-tree $T^+$ as a minor, where $T^+$ is the tree obtained by adding a universal vertex to $T$, there exists a graph $H$ with treewidth at most $4h-1$ such that $G$ is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{2(t-1)d}$. The bound on the treewidth of $H$ is best possible up to a factor $2$, and improves on a $2^{h+2}-4$ bound that follows from a recent result of Dujmović, Hickingbotham, Hodor, Joret, La, Micek, Morin, Rambaud, and Wood (2024).

papers.title: Connectivity notions on compatible digraphs in equational classes
papers.summary:
A digraph $\mathbb G$ is called weakly connected, strongly connected, and extremely connected if any two vertices of $\mathbb G$ are connected respectively by an oriented, a directed, and a symmetric path in $\mathbb G$. We investigate the algebraic properties of digraphs that force some of these connectivity notions to coincide.
  We prove that for digraphs with a Hobby-McKenzie polymorphism, the strong and the extreme components coincide. Conversely, if the strong and the extreme components of any compatible digraph in an equational class of algebras coincide, then the class must have a Hobby-McKenzie term. As a consequence, we obtain that an equational class $\mathcal V$ is $n$-permutable for some $n$ if and only if the weak components of any compatible reflexive digraph in $\mathcal V$ are extremely connected.

papers.title: Super Major Index Cyclic Sieving
papers.summary: Recently, Armon and Swanson introduced signed standard tableaux and a corresponding super major index that refines the classical major index. In this paper, we prove that signed standard tableaux of rectangular shape exhibit a cyclic sieving phenomenon (CSP) under the combined action of Schützenberger promotion and cyclic shift of the signs, with the sieving polynomial given by the super major index generating function. This extends Rhoades's celebrated CSP for standard Young tableaux. Furthermore, by considering Cartesian products of tableaux, we generalize this result to arbitrary non-rectangular shapes.

papers.title: Periodic points and residual finiteness of automorphism groups of subshifts
papers.summary: If totally periodic points are dense in a subshift $X$, its automorphism group is residually finite. We show a weak converse: if periodic points are not dense in a subshift $X$, then the automorphism group of $X \times Y$ is not residually finite for full shifts $Y$ (and sufficiently full-shift-like subshifts). On the other hand, we show that the automorphism group of a block gluing $\Z^2$-subshift is always locally embeddable in finite groups (thus sofic). Hochman recently constructed a strongly irreducible $\Z^2$-subshift with no periodic points. Combining our result with this example gives a strongly irreducible $\Z^2$-subshift whose automorphism group is not residually finite, which solves a question of Coornaert and Ceccherini-Silberstein.

papers.title: Geometric constructions for Steinitz-type bounds in dimension two
papers.summary: We investigate inequalities for partial sums of complex numbers with bounded modulus and zero total sum, a topic referred to as "polygonal confinement". Starting from Steinitz's classical result, we provide detailed constructions yielding explicit bounds, including $\sqrt{5}$, $\sqrt{3}$, $2$, and $\sqrt{2}$, depending on geometric constraints or weighted settings. The proofs are fully detailed with step-by-step constructions of permutations, highlighting the combinatorial and geometric intuition. We conclude with conjectures on optimal universal constants and directions for future research.

papers.title: An Extension of Major-Minor Mean Field Game Theory
papers.summary: This work extends the theory presented in Mean Field Games with a Dominating Player by Bensoussan, Chau and Yam on mean field games with a dominating player, to the case in which the utility and cost functions depend not only on the law of the states, but on the joint state--control law. We incorporate the conditional distribution of the state--control pair of the representative agent given the common noise of the dominating player. In addition, we generalize the role of the dominating player to include the direct impact of its controls $u_0$ on the dynamics and functionals of the system. The optimization problems are reformulated in terms of the conditional distribution of the state--control pair, the necessary optimality conditions are established via stochastic maximum principles, and a coupled SHJB--FP system of equations is obtained that synthesizes the equilibrium conditions. This framework provides a significant extension of the existing literature on MFG with a dominating player.

papers.title: On the number of non-cyclic subgroups of finite p-groups
papers.summary: Let $G$ be a finite $p$-group and $δ(G)$ denote the number of all non-cyclic subgroups of $G$. In this paper, an upper bound for $δ(G)$ is obtained. Furthermore, we prove that $δ(G)\leq δ(M_p(1, 1, 1) \times C_{p}^{n-3})$ (if $p=2$, then $δ(G)\leq δ(D_8\times C_{2}^{n-3})$), for any non-elementary abelian $p$-group $G$ of order $p^n$.

papers.title: Robust transitivity without sectional-hyperbolicity
papers.summary: For any integer $n \geq 5$, we construct an $n$-dimensional $C^1$ vector field exhibiting a robustly transitive singular attractor which is not sectional-hyperbolic. Nevertheless, the attractor is singular-hyperbolic. This provides the first such examples improving some features of the constructions in [17, 32].

papers.title: Yet another characterization of Birkhoff Billiards inside discs
papers.summary: In this short paper, we show a characterization of Birkhoff Billiards inside discs which is related to the expansion of the formal Lazutkin conjugacy at the boundary.

papers.title: Tangent measures of self-similar sets satisfying the strong separation condition
papers.summary: This paper investigates tangent measures in the sense of Preiss for self-similar sets on ${\mathbb{R}^d}$ that satisfy the strong separation condition. Through the dynamics of ``zooming in'' on any typical point, we derive an explicit and uniform formula for the tangent measures associated with this category of self-similar sets on ${\mathbb{R}^d}$. Furthermore, for any self-similar set $C\subset{\mathbb{R}^d}$ under the open set condition instead of the strong separation condition, we find that the support of any tangent measure at each point $x\in C$ is one of the limit models at that point. Conversely, any limit model at each point $x\in C$ is the support of one of the tangent measures at that point.

papers.title: The largest $K_r$-free set of vertices in a random graph
papers.summary: For $r \ge 2$ and a graph $G$, let $α_{r}(G)$ be the maximum number of vertices in a $K_r$-free subgraph of $G$. We investigate the value $α_{r}(G)$ when $G$ is the random graph $G \sim G_{n, 1/2}$ and discover the following phenomenon: with high probability, $α_r(G)$ lies in an interval of constant length that varies in a non-monotonic fashion from $1$ to $\lfloor r/2\rfloor+1$ depending on the value of $n$. The special case $r=2$ corresponds to the independence number of random graphs which is well-known to have two-point concentration; our results therefore extend and generalize this basic fact in random graph theory, showing more complicated behavior when $r>2$. We also prove similar results where $K_r$ is replaced by any color critical graph like $C_5$.

papers.title: Double Italian domination in trees
papers.summary: Let $G$ be a graph with vertex set $V=V(G)$. A double Roman dominating function on a graph $G$ is a function $f : V \to \{0,1,2,3\}$ satisfying the conditions that if $f(v) = 0$, then vertex $v$ must have at least two neighbors in $V_2$ or one neighbor in $V_3$, if $f(v) = 1$, then vertex $v$ must have at least one neighbor in $V_2 \cup V_3$. The weight of a double Roman dominating function $f$ is the sum $f(V) = \sum_{v \in V} f(v)$, and the double Roman domination number $γ_{dR}(G)$ is the minimum weight of a double Roman dominating function on $G$. A double Italian dominating function on a graph $G$ is a function $f : V \to \{0,1,2,3\}$ satisfying the condition that for every vertex $u \in V$, if $f(u) \in \{0,1\}$, then $\sum_{v \in N[u]} f(v) \ge 3$. The double Roman domination number $γ_{dI}(G)$ is the minimum weight of a double Italian dominating function on $G$. Mojdeh and Volkmann [D.A. Mojdeh and L. Volkmann, Roman {3}-domination (double Italian domination), Discrete Appl. Math. 283 (2020), 555--564] proved that $γ_{dI}(T) = γ_{dR}(T)$ for any tree $T$. However, we find that there is a minor issue in the proof. In this paper, we first prove that $γ_{dI}(T) \neq γ_{dR}(T)$. Subsequently, we present a sharp bound on the double Italian domination number of any non-trivial tree $T$, and characterize the trees attaining this bound.

papers.title: An Infinite Family of 6_Regular B-Cayley Graphs from the Petersen Graph
papers.summary: We construct an infinite family of 6-regular graphs $\{G_n\}_{n\ge 3}$ by taking $n$ copies of the Petersen graph and wiring corresponding vertices according to an $n$-cycle permutation. Each $G_n$ has $10n$ vertices, $30n$ edges, and automorphism group $D_{5n}$ of order $10n$, acting with two vertex orbits of size $5n$. The graphs have girth $4$ and diameter $\lfloor n/2\rfloor+2$. We prove that $G_3$ and $G_4$ are Ramanujan graphs, satisfying $|λ_2| \le 2\sqrt{5}$. The first five members ($n=3,\dots,7$) have been deposited in the House of Graphs database as entries 56324--56328. This construction provides new examples of highly symmetric regular graphs and contributes two new Ramanujan graphs to the literature. All computational scripts are available online for full reproducibility.

papers.title: Bridging Classical Sensitivity and Quantum Scrambling: A Tutorial on Out-of-Time-Ordered Correlators
papers.summary: In classical dynamical systems, chaotic behavior is often associated with exponential sensitivity to initial conditions together with global phase-space structure. Translating this geometric concept to the strictly linear framework of quantum mechanics presents a conceptual puzzle. The out-of-time-ordered correlator (OTOC) is often motivated as the quantum analogue of the classical butterfly effect, but this slogan can hide important mathematical distinctions. This tutorial bridges the gap between applied mathematics and quantum information by detailing the mathematical machinery of the OTOC. We explore how classical sensitivity translates to operator non-commutativity, why standard two-point correlation functions fail to cleanly detect this sensitivity, and how the delocalization of quantum observables relates to classical notions of mixing. Crucially, we outline what the OTOC can and cannot diagnose, distinguishing between local instability and global chaos. Ultimately, we provide a precise and usable conceptual map, exploring how the Koopman-von Neumann formalism offers a framework to view classical and quantum dynamics through a shared linear perspective.

papers.title: Monoidal categories graded by partial commutative monoids
papers.summary: Effectful categories have two classes of morphisms: pure morphisms, which form a monoidal category; and effectful morphisms, which can only be combined monoidally with central morphisms (such as the pure ones), forming a premonoidal category. This suggests seeing morphisms of an effectful category as carrying a grade that combines under the monoidal product in a partially defined manner. We axiomatize this idea with the notion of monoidal category graded by a partial commutative monoid (PCM). Monoidal categories arise as the special case of grading by the singleton PCM, and effectful categories arise from grading by a two-element PCM. Further examples include grading by powerset PCMs, modelling non-interfering parallelism for programs accessing shared resources, and grading by intervals, modelling bounded resource usage. We show that effectful categories form a coreflective subcategory of PCM-graded monoidal categories; introduce cartesian structure, recovering Freyd categories; and describe PCM-graded monoidal categories as monoids by viewing a PCM as a thin promonoidal category.

papers.title: Geometric structures of $G$-fans associated with rank $3$ cluster-cyclic exchange matrices
papers.summary: In this paper, we investigate the geometric structures of $G$-fans associated with rank $3$ real cluster-cyclic exchange matrices. In this class, a simple recursion for tropical signs was found, which enables us to study the detailed properties of $c$-, $g$-vectors. We introduce two kinds of upper bounds of the $G$-fans. The first one is the global upper bound, which comes from a hyperbolic surface containing all $g$-vectors after an initial mutation. The second one is the local upper bound, which reflects the internal separateness structure. As applications, we prove that there is no periodicity among $g$-vectors, and we completely determine the sign of $g$-vectors. We also prove the monotonicity of $g$-vectors under the minimum assumption. Moreover, we show that the three global upper bounds can be simplified to a single uniform upper bound.

papers.title: Three-Dimensional Affine Spatial Logics
papers.summary: We focus on a branch of region-based spatial logics dealing with affine geometry. The research on this topic is scarce: only a handful of papers investigate such systems, mostly in the case of the real plane. Our long-term goal is to analyse certain family of affine logics with inclusion and convexity as primitives interpreted over real spaces of increasing dimensionality. In this article we show that logics of different dimensionalities must have different theories, thus justifying further work on different dimensions. We then focus on the three-dimensional case, exploring the expressiveness of this logic and consequently showing that it is possible to construct formulas describing a three-dimensional coordinate frame. The final result, making use of the high expressive power of this logic, is that every region satisfies an affine complete formula, meaning that all regions satisfying it are affine equivalent.

papers.title: Polytopes with large transversal ratio
papers.summary: The transversal ratio of a polytope $P$ is the minimum proportion of vertices of $P$ required to intersect each facet of $P$. The weak chromatic number of $P$ is the minimum number of colors required to color the vertices of $P$ so that no facet is monochromatic. We will construct an infinite family of $d$-polytopes for each $d\geq 5$ whose transversal ratio approaches 1 as the number of vertices grows. In particular, this implies that the weak chromatic number for $d$-polytopes is unbounded for each $d\geq 5$. The previous best known lower bounds on the supremum of the transversal ratio for $d$-polytopes for $d\geq 5$ were 2/5 for odd $d$ by Novik and Zheng, and 1/2 for even $d$ by Holmsen, Pach, and Tverberg. In the case of simplicial $(d-1)$-spheres, the best known lower bounds were 1/2 for $d=5$ and $6/11$ for $d=6$ by Novik and Zheng.

papers.title: An Explicit Bound for the Conjugator Length Function of a Surface Group
papers.summary: For a surface group $π_1(Σ_g)=\langle c_1,\dots , c_{2g}\mid c_1\cdots c_{2g}c_1^{-1}\cdots c_{2g}^{-1}\rangle$ with genus $g\geq 2$, we provide an explicit bound $n-1\leq \mathrm{CL}(2n)=\mathrm{CL}(2n+1)\leq n+8g-1$ for the conjugator length function $\mathrm{CL}:\mathbb N\to\mathbb N$ of $π_1(Σ_g)$ via a detailed analysis of conjugation reductions.

papers.title: Classification of unmixed parity binomial edge ideals of cactus and chordal graphs
papers.summary: In this article, we characterize all unmixed and Cohen-Macaulay parity binomial edge ideals of cactus and chordal graphs in terms of the structural properties of the graph.

papers.title: Dyadic Self-Similarity in a Perturbed Hofstadter $Q$-Recursion
papers.summary:
We study a perturbed variant of Hofstadter's $Q$-recursion \[ Q(n)=Q(n-Q(n-1))+Q(n-Q(n-2))+(-1)^n, \qquad Q(1)=Q(2)=1 . \] Numerical experiments indicate that the sequence remains well defined for very large values of $n$ and exhibits an unexpectedly structured large-scale behavior. The data provide strong empirical evidence that the sequence grows approximately linearly, with \[ Q(n)\approx \frac{n}{2}. \] Writing $Q(n)=n/2+E(n)$, the fluctuation term $E(n)$ appears to display a persistent dyadic self-similarity: characteristic patterns recur across scales related by powers of two. A heuristic analysis of the recursion suggests a possible explanation for this phenomenon. Since the recursive indices typically lie close to $n/2$, the dynamics repeatedly couple values at scale $n$ with values near scale $n/2$, producing an effective dyadic renormalization mechanism.
  We further analyze the associated index processes $t_1(n)=n-Q(n-1)$ and $t_2(n)=n-Q(n-2)$, which reveal a pronounced parity dependence in the dynamics. In addition, numerical experiments on the frequency sequence of the values of $Q(n)$ suggest a regular dyadic organization with approximately geometric multiplicities inside blocks $B_k={2^k,\dots,2^{k+1}-1}$.
  Taken together, these observations point to a possible parity-split dyadic renormalization structure governing the long-term dynamics of the recursion. Establishing rigorous results for these phenomena remains an open problem.

papers.title: Hypergraph Ramsey numbers with quasipolynomial growth rate
papers.summary: For a 3-uniform hypergraph (3-graph) $F$, let $r(F,n)$ be the smallest $N$ such that any $N$-vertex $F$-free 3-graph has an independent set of size $n$. We construct a $3$-graph $H_2$ with six vertices and five edges such that $r(H_2,n)=n^{Θ(\log n)}$, and a more general family of $3$-graphs $F$ for which $r(F,n)=n^{\log^{Θ(1)}(n)}$. These are the first examples of such Ramsey number known to be neither polynomial nor exponential.

papers.title: On the Extension Theorem for Packing Steiner Forests
papers.summary: We consider the problem of packing edge-disjoint Steiner forests in a graph. The input consists of a multi-graph $G=(V,E)$ and a collection of $h$ vertex subsets $S = \{S_1,S_2,\ldots,S_h\}$. A Steiner forest for $S$, also called an $S$-forest, is a forest of $G$ in which each $S_i$ is connected. In the case where $h=1$, this is the Steiner Tree packing problem. Kriesell's conjecture postulates that $2k$-edge-connectivity of $S_1$ is sufficient to find $k$ edge-disjoint $S_1$-trees. Lau showed that $24k$-edge-connectivity suffices for the Steiner Tree packing problem, which was improved to $6.5k$ by West and Wu and $5k+4$ by Devos, McDonald and Pivotto. In his thesis, Lau asserts that for the Steiner Forest problem, if each $S_i$ is $30k$-edge-connected in $G$, then there exist $k$ edge-disjoint $S$-forests. However, Lau's proof relies on an intermediate theorem called the Extension Theorem, which in this paper we will demonstrate has a gap by providing a counterexample to Lau's Extension Theorem. Furthermore, we will resolve this gap by correcting Lau's proof to show that $36k$-edge-connectivity of each $S_i$ suffices to pack $k$ $S$-forests. More careful analysis yields that $35k$-edge-connectivity of each $S_i$ is sufficient when $k \geq 8$.

papers.title: Compact Invariant Random Subgroups
papers.summary: We study ergodic invariant random subgroups that give full measure to the subset of compact subgroups. We show that in real Lie groups, compactly generated $p$-adic Lie groups, locally compact hyperbolic groups and infinitely ended groups they are always contained in a compact normal subgroup. In general $p$-adic Lie groups, we show they are contained in the locally elliptic radical. In totally disconnected locally compact groups, we show they are contained in the intersection of all Levi subgroups of inner automorphisms.

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17188v1.title: 正則言語の逐次密度
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17188v1.summary: 本論文では、確率測度の列に対する正則言語（rational language）の密度の概念を導入しています。ベルヌーイ測度の列 μₙ が正のベルヌーイ測度 μ̄ に収束する場合、その逐次密度は μ̄ に関する通常の密度と一致することを証明しています。また、不変確率測度の列が不変確率測度 μ̄ に強収束する場合、すべての正則言語についてその列における逐次密度が存在することを示しています。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17188v1.appendix: 本論文は automata theory および symbolic dynamics の分野に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17091v1.title: 距離平均次元における量子化と古典的変分原理について
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17091v1.summary: コンパクト距離空間上の同相写像に対し、不変確率測度上の測度論的ε-エントロピー写像を用いた距離平均次元の定式化が知られている。本論文では、極限(ε → 0⁺)と上限(sup_{μ})の操作順序が交換可能かという問題に対し、「平均量子化次元(mean quantization dimension)」という概念を導入した。主結果として、距離平均次元が平均量子化次元の最大値と一致すること(mdim̄_M(X, d, T) = max_{μ ∈ P_T(X)} mdim̄_Q(μ))を証明した。また、既存のエントロピー写像の一部はこの性質を満たさず、古典的変分原理に従わないことを示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17091v1.appendix: 本論文は、興味ある分野のうち symbolic dynamics に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17084v1.title: ランク2の自由因子複体（Free Factor Complex）のモデル理論について
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17084v1.summary: 有限ランクの自由群における自由因子複体（Free Factor Complex）の性質をモデル理論の観点から調査した研究である。ランク2の場合に焦点を当て、その理論の公理化を行うとともに、その理論が ω-安定（ω-stable）であることを示した。また、その素モデル（prime model）が AF₂ であることを明らかにしている。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17084v1.appendix: 本論文は自由群の構造やその複体に関連する性質を扱っているため、興味のある分野のうち「structure of large countable infinite group」に深く関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17029v1.title: ちょうど5つの固有値を持つ例を含むNeumaierグラフの族
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17029v1.summary: Neumaierグラフは、正則クリークを持つエッジ正則グラフであり、長年の未解決問題として「ちょうど5つの固有値を持つNeumaierグラフが存在するか」という問いがあった。本論文では、パラメータ(v, k, λ; e, s) = (48, 14, 2; 1, 4)を持つ1063個の非同型なNeumaierグラフの族を提示した。このうち25個のグラフがちょうど5つの固有値を持つことを示し、上述の未解決問題を解決するとともに、このパラメータにおける最初のNeumaierグラフの構成例を与えた。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17029v1.appendix: 本論文はグラフ理論および組合せ論（combinatorics）に関する研究であり、指定された興味ある分野の中では、広い意味で combinatorics on words などの組合せ論的対象に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16850v1.title: 最適化とダイナミクスの統合による逐次計算の並列化：逐次処理のボトルネックを打破する並列ニュートン法へのガイド
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16850v1.summary: 大規模言語モデルやGPUの普及に伴い並列アルゴリズムの重要性が高まっているが、RNNやMCMCのような動的システムは逐次処理のボトルネックが課題であった。本論文では、動的システムの評価を非線形方程式系として再定義し、並列アソシエイティブ・スキャンを用いたニュートン法で解く手法を提案している。従来の並列ニュートン法における非効率性や不安定性、収束保証の欠如という課題に対し、準ニュートン法や信頼領域法を導入することでスケーラビリティと安定性を向上させた。理論面では、ピカール反復やヤコビ反復を統合し、近似精度と安定性に依存する線形収束率を確立した。さらに、並列化の加速条件を動的システムの最大リアプノフ指数（Largest Lyapunov Exponent）によって特徴付け、並列ニュートン法が有効に機能する理論的根拠を提示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16850v1.appendix: 本論文は動的システムの安定性を解析するために最大リアプノフ指数を用いており、複雑な動的挙動を扱う「complex dynamics」の分野と理論的な関連性があります。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16819v1.title: 木の自己同型群の非許容表現
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16819v1.summary: 正則局所有限木の自己同型群において、非許容的（inadmissible）な既約バナッハ表現が存在することが示されている。また、滑らかなベクトルからなる稠密な部分空間が、代数的既約成分を一切含まないことが明らかにされている。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16819v1.appendix: この論文は、巨大な無限群の構造を扱う「structure of large countable infinite group」の分野に関連しています。木の自己同型群という無限群の表現論的性質を論じているためです。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16814v1.title: 完全剰余自由な Demushkin 群の再考
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16814v1.summary: 2次元の pro-p Poincaré 双対群である Demushkin 群のクラスにおいて、pro-p 極限群（pro-p limit groups）に該当する例および該当しない例を新たに確立した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16814v1.appendix: 本論文は pro-p 群という無限群の構造を扱っており、興味ある分野の中では「structure of large countable infinite group」の理論的周辺領域に関連しています。2次元の pro-p Poincaré 双対群の分類と極限群の性質に焦点を当てています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16771v1.title: 斜左ブレース（skew left braces）の交換確率
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16771v1.summary: 本論文では、群論における概念を類推し、斜左ブレース（skew left brace）の交換確率という概念を導入している。有限の非自明な斜左ブレースにおいて、交換確率の上限が3/4であることを示し、開区間(5/8, 1)には3/4を除いて交換確率が存在しないことを証明した。また、交換確率が3/4または5/8となる斜左ブレースの特性評価を行い、交換確率が65/128より大きい有限斜左ブレースは必ずべき零（nilpotent）になることを示した。さらに、この確率は斜左ブレースのイソクリニズム（isoclinism）の下で不変であることを証明した。加えて、コンパクト・ハウスドルフ位相同相な斜左ブレース B において、有限の中心化指数を持つ要素の集合がボレル部分群になることを示した。無限の非自明な斜左ブレースにおいても上限は3/4であり、開区間(5/8, 1)で交換確率として現れる有理数は3/4のみであることを明らかにしている。
keywords: compact Hausdorff topological skew left brace
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16771v1.appendix: 本論文は無限の斜左ブレースやその部分群の構造を扱っており、興味ある分野のうち「structure of large countable infinite group」に関連する内容を含んでいます。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16763v1.title: コヒーレントなRFRS群
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16763v1.summary: コホモロジー次元が2以下の有限生成virtually RFRS群において、群がコヒーレントであること、第2 L²-ベッチ数が0であること、および群がvirtually free-by-cyclicであることが互いに同値であることを証明した。第2 L²-ベッチ数の非消滅性は、広範な群のクラスにおけるコヒーレンス（有限生成部分群がすべて有限表示可能である性質）に対する初の全域的な障害となり、具体的な反例を示さずに不整合性を証明することを可能にする。この結果の応用として、2次元コクセター群のコヒーレンスを完全に特徴付け、非正の欠数を持つ群において不整合性が一般的であるという予想を解決した。また、virtually compact special群においてコヒーレンスがアルゴリズム的に判定可能であり、準等長、測度同値、および副有限不変量であることを示した。付録では、cubulated locally quasi-convex hyperbolic群がvirtually free-by-cyclicであることが示されている。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16763v1.appendix: 本論文は、提示された興味ある分野のうち「structure of large countable infinite group」に深く関連しています。RFRS群やコクセター群といった無限群の構造的性質、およびL²-ベッチ数やコヒーレンスといった幾何学的群論における重要な不変量を扱っているためです。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16983v1.title: 側方流動に対する木ベースの機械学習モデルの形式検証
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16983v1.summary: 地盤工学のハザード予測における機械学習モデルが、物理的に不整合な予測を行う問題を解決するため、本論文では、学習済み決定木アンサンブルをSatisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) ソルバ上の論理式として符号化し、入力領域全体で物理的仕様を満たすか検証する手法を提案している。2011年カンタベリー地震のデータセットを用いた評価では、XGBoostやEBMに対して水位やPGAの単調性などの仕様を検証し、反例の検出や物理的一貫性の証明が可能であることを示した。また、SHAP等の事後説明手法では物理的違反を見逃す可能性があることを指摘し、検証と修正を繰り返すエンジニアリングループの重要性を提唱している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16983v1.appendix: 本論文はSMTソルバを用いた形式検証を扱っており、興味ある分野のうち「automata theory」や「formal languages」に関連する計算機科学的体系（形式手法）を地盤工学に応用した研究と言えます。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16695v1.title: グラフの独立多項式
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16695v1.summary: 本論文では、有限単純グラフGの独立多項式P_G(x)について、x=-1における値、対称性、および辺イデアルのh-多項式との関連性を研究している。大きなスターグラフ（big star graphs）を対象に、P_G(-1)が0, 1, -1をとる条件や、pseudo-Gorenstein* メンバーの特徴付けを行った。また、グラフに葉を付加した際の独立多項式の明示的な公式を導出し、対称性を持つための必要十分条件が各頂点にちょうど2つの葉を付加することであることを特定した。さらに、これらの結果をキャタピラーグラフへ応用したほか、余弦グラフ（cochordal graphs）における対称な独立多項式の分類、および独立数が小さいn頂点連結グラフのP_G(-1)の値の範囲を決定している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16695v1.appendix: 本論文は組合せ論（Combinatorics）の分野、特にグラフ理論と代数的組合せ論に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16674v1.title: 自由積および自由因子の副有限検出
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16674v1.summary: 辺群が実質巡回（virtually cyclic）であるような、有限生成の実質自由群のグラフの基本群 G について、G が剰余有限であればコホモロジー的に good であることを示した。さらに G が LERF（Locally Extended Residually Finite）である場合、G が非自明な自由積として分解することとその副有限完備化 Ĝ が非自明な自由副有限積として分解することが同値であることを証明した。また、Ĝ から G の one-ended な自由因子を検出することも可能である。応用として、有限生成自由群における副有限硬性（profinitely rigid）を持つ語は、普遍的に副有限硬性を持つことが導かれる。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16674v1.appendix: この論文は「structure of large countable infinite group」に関連しています。グラフの基本群やその副有限完備化、自由積への分解といった無限群の構造的性質を扱っているためです。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16670v1.title: 大きな最大次数を持つグラフに対する二次の時間計算量の彩色アルゴリズム
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16670v1.summary: グラフ彩色はグラフ理論における中心的な問題であり、一般のグラフに対してはNP困難である。本論文ではBorodin–Kostochka予想に着想を得て、大きな最大次数Δを持ちサイズΔのクリークを含まないグラフの彩色問題を研究した。その結果、このようなグラフに対して(Δ-1)彩色を構築する二次の時間計算量のアルゴリズムを提示した。また、最大次数Δ ≥ 7.3 × 10⁹かつクリーク数ω(G) < Δを満たすすべてのグラフGにおいて、彩色数χ(G) ≤ Δ - 1が成立することを証明した。これはReedによる長年の結果を改善するものである。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16670v1.appendix: 本論文はグラフ理論および離散数学を扱っており、興味ある分野の中では「combinatorics on words」に関連の深い「combinatorics（組合せ論）」の領域に該当します。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16591v1.title: サブシフトの自己同型群における周期点と剰余有限性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16591v1.summary: サブシフトXにおいて全周期点が稠密であれば、その自己同型群は剰余有限となる。本論文ではその弱い逆を証明し、Xにおいて周期点が稠密でない場合、フルシフトYとの直積X×Yの自己同型群は剰余有限にならないことを示した。一方で、ブロック接着性を持つZ²サブシフトの自己同型群は、常に有限群に局所埋め込み可能（したがってsofic）であることを示した。また、Hochmanが構成した周期点を持たない強既約なZ²サブシフトの例と本結果を組み合わせることで、自己同型群が剰余有限ではない強既約Z²サブシフトの存在を導き、CoornaertおよびCeccherini-Silbersteinによる問いを解決した。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16591v1.appendix: 本論文は、symbolic dynamicsにおける自己同型群の代数的構造（特に剰余有限性やLEF性）を扱っており、興味ある分野であるsymbolic dynamicsおよびstructure of large countable infinite groupに深く関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16468v2.title: 円板内におけるBirkhoffビリヤードのさらなる特徴付け
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16468v2.summary: 本論文では、境界における形式的Lazutkin共役の展開に関連した、円板内におけるBirkhoffビリヤードの新たな特徴付けを提示する。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16468v2.appendix: 本論文はビリヤード系という力学系の一種を扱っており、提示された興味ある分野の中ではcomplex dynamics（複素力学系）やsymbolic dynamics（記号力学系）と広義の力学系の文脈で関連する可能性があります。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16467v1.title: 強分離条件を満たす自己相似集合の接測度
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16467v1.summary: 本論文では、Rᵈ上の強分離条件を満たす自己相似集合を対象に、Preissの意味での接測度を調査している。典型的な点に対して「ズームイン」を行う力学系の手法を用いることで、この種の自己相似集合に関連する接測度の明示的かつ一様な公式を導出した。また、強分離条件の代わりに開集合条件を満たす自己相似集合Cについても検討を行い、各点xにおける任意の接測度の台がその点における極限モデルの1つであること、およびその逆が成立することを示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16467v1.appendix: 本論文は、自己相似集合の解析に力学的な手法を用いているため、興味ある分野のうち symbolic dynamics や complex dynamics と関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16234v1.title: 曲面群の共役長関数に対する明示的な境界
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16234v1.summary: 種数 g ≥ 2 の曲面群 π₁(Σ_g) = ⟨c₁, ..., c₂g | c₁ ... c₂g c₁⁻¹ ... c₂g⁻¹⟩ において、共役縮小の精緻な分析を通じて、共役長関数 CL: ℕ → ℕ に対する明示的な境界 n - 1 ≤ CL(2n) = CL(2n + 1) ≤ n + 8g - 1 を与えた。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16234v1.appendix: この論文は「structure of large countable infinite group」の分野に関連しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16111v1.title: 摂動を加えた Hofstadter Q-再帰における二進自己相似性
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16111v1.summary: 本論文では、Hofstadter の Q-再帰に (-1)ⁿ という摂動を加えたバリアントについて調査している。数値実験の結果、この数列は大きな n に対しても定義可能であり、Q(n) ≈ n/2 という線形な成長を示す。変動項 E(n) = Q(n) - n/2 は、2のべき乗のスケール間で再帰的なパターンが現れる「二進自己相似性」を呈しており、再帰インデックスが n/2 付近に集中することによる二進繰り込みメカニズムの可能性を提示している。また、インデックスプロセスの解析からパリティ依存性が明らかになり、値の頻度分布もブロック Bₖ = {2ᵏ, ..., 2ᵏ⁺¹ - 1} 内で幾何学的な多重度を持つ二進構造を示唆している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16111v1.appendix: 数値解析による動的な構造の解明という点において、validated numerics との関連性が見出されます。また、数列の自己相似的な振る舞いや繰り込み構造は、symbolic dynamics や combinatorics on words の手法が応用される可能性を示唆しています。

http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16022v1.title: コンパクトな不変ランダム部分群
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16022v1.summary: コンパクトな部分群の集合上に全測度を持つエルゴード的不変ランダム部分群(Invariant Random Subgroups; IRS)を研究している。実Lie群、コンパクト生成p進Lie群、局所コンパクト双曲的群、および無限の端を持つ群において、これらのIRSは常にコンパクト正規部分群に含まれることを示した。また、一般的なp進Lie群ではlocally elliptic radicalに含まれ、完全不連結局所コンパクト群では内部自己同型写像のすべてのLevi部分群の共通部分に含まれることを示している。
http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16022v1.appendix: 本論文は局所コンパクト群やp進Lie群の構造を扱っており、指定された興味ある分野のうち「structure of large countable infinite group」に関連する内容を含んでいます。

papers.title: A Simple Categorical Calculus of Interacting Processes
papers.summary: We present a calculus that models a simple sort of process interaction. Our calculus consists of a collection of terms together with a rewrite relation, parameterised by an arbitrary multicategory whose morphisms we understand as non-interactive processes. We show that our calculus is confluent and terminating, and that terms modulo the induced convertibility relation form a virtual double category. We relate our calculus to the free cornering of a monoidal category, which is a double-categorical model of process interaction that is similar in spirit to the calculus presented herein. Precisely, we construct a functor from the virtual double category given by our calculus into the underlying virtual double category of the free cornering of the free monoidal category on the multicategory of non-interacting processes. If we think of the terms of our calculus as programs and the rewriting system as an operational semantics for these programs, this functor gives a sound denotational semantics for our calculus in terms of the free cornering.

papers.title: Counting Strict Gridlock on Graphs
papers.summary: Graph colorings have been of interest to mathematicians for a long time, but relatively recently, social scientists have also found them to be interesting tools for studying group behavior. In the last 20 years, scientists have begun to study how coloring problems can be solved by groups of individuals on a graph, which has led to new insights into network structure, group dynamics, and individual human behavior. Despite this newfound utility, the exact nature of these distributed coloring problems is not well-understood, and established mathematical tools like the chromatic polynomial miss the unique challenges that arise in these social problem-solving situations with limited information. In this paper, we provide a new framework for understanding these distributed problems by defining a new kind of graph coloring with particular relevance to consensus formation on networks, in which all vertices are trying to agree on a common color. These strict gridlock colorings represent roadblocks to consensus where the group will not reach a uniform coloring using natural update processes. We describe a recurrence relation that provides an algorithm for counting these gridlocked colorings, which establishes a mathematical measure of how much a given graph hinders consensus in a group.

papers.title: On the K-theory of matroids with Tutte coverings
papers.summary: The aim of this work is to explicitly compute the K-theory of the category of matroids with respect to the covering family of Tutte coverings. In particular, we show that this is equivalent to the K-theory spectrum of the category of graphic matroids on looped forests, with the covering family generated by isomorphisms. Further, we show that this yields an equivalence of $C_2$-spectra.

papers.title: Biregular bipartite labeled multigraphs and perfect matchings in bipartite tensor products
papers.summary: In 2019, P. Higgins formulated [1] a question about bipartite graphs (see Conjecture 1 below); this question arises in the study of regular finite semigroups. F. V. Petrov formulated [2] another combinatorial conjecture (Conjecture 3); Conjecture 3 implies Conjecture 1 and seems simple itself. However, both conjectures remain unproven in the general case. In the present paper, some special cases are proved, Conjecture 4 is formulated in the same spirit, and some of its special cases are proved. In addition, Conjecture 1 is reduced to a matrix inequality (Conjecture 5); this inequality is, in turn, also proved in a special case.

papers.title: Finite Cardinalities of Misère Quotients
papers.summary: We find that partisan misère quotients can have any finite cardinality other than 3, answering a question of Allen. This contrasts with impartial misère quotients, which must have even cardinality.

papers.title: Geometric approach to the modular isomorphism problem: groups of order 64
papers.summary: We introduce a procedure based on computational algebraic geometry to determine whether two algebras are isomorphic. We then apply it to show that if $R$ is a commutative unital ring in which $2$ is not invertible, $G$ is a group of order dividing $64$ and $H$ some group, then an isomorphism of unital algebras $RG \cong RH$ implies an isomorphism of groups $G \cong H$.

papers.title: Around Gromov's injectivity lemma and applications to post-injunctive groups
papers.summary: Gottschalk's surjunctivity conjecture states that for all group universes and finite alphabets, every equivariant and continuous selfmap of the full shift, known as cellular automaton, cannot be a strict embedding. Not all surjective cellular automata are injective. However, if the surjectivity condition is replaced by a certain strengthened property called post-surjectivity then all post-surjective cellular automata must be bijective whenever the universe is a sofic group. A group universe is said to be post-injunctive if every post-surjective cellular automaton with finite alphabet over this group universe must be bijective.
